questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The number of values of $\mathrm{x}$ in the interval $[0,3 \pi]$ satisfying $2 \sin ^2 x+5 \sin ^2 x-3=0$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & 2 \sin ^2 x+5 \sin x-3=0 \\ & \Rightarrow(\sin x+3)(2 \sin x-1)=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \sin x=-3 \text { or } \sin x=\frac{1}{2} \\ & \Rightarrow \text { no solution or } \sin x=\sin \frac{\pi}{6} \\ & \Rightarrow x=n \pi+(-1) n \frac{\pi}{6}\end{aligned}$
But $x \in[0,3 \pi]$
$$
\Rightarrow x=\frac{\pi}{6}, \frac{5 \pi}{6}, \frac{13 \pi}{6}, \frac{17 \pi}{6}
$$
four solution
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of $\sin 18^{\circ}$ is
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \sin 90^{\circ}=\sin 5\left(81^{\circ}\right) \text { and let } 18^{\circ}=\mathrm{A} \\
& \sin 90^{\circ}=\sin 5 \mathrm{~A}=\sin (3 \mathrm{~A}+2 \mathrm{~A}) \\
& \therefore 90^{\circ}=3 \mathrm{~A}+2 \mathrm{~A} \Rightarrow \sin \left(90^{\circ}-3 \mathrm{~A}\right)=\sin 2 \mathrm{~A} \\
& \therefore \sin 2 \mathrm{~A}-\cos 3 \mathrm{~A} \Rightarrow 2 \sin \mathrm{A} \cos \mathrm{A}=4 \cos ^3 \mathrm{~A}-3 \cos \mathrm{A} \\
& \therefore \cos \mathrm{A}\left(2 \sin \mathrm{A}-4 \cos ^2 \mathrm{~A}+3\right)=0 \\
& \therefore \cos \mathrm{A}=0 \text { or }\left[2 \sin \mathrm{A}-4\left(1-\sin ^2 \mathrm{~A}\right)+3\right]=0 \\
& \therefore \mathrm{A}=\frac{\pi}{2} \text { or } 4 \sin ^2 \mathrm{~A}+2 \sin \mathrm{A}-1=0
\end{aligned}
$$
Since, $A=18^{\circ}, A \neq \frac{\pi}{2}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore 4 \sin ^2 \mathrm{~A}+2 \sin \mathrm{A}-1=0 \\
& \therefore \sin \mathrm{A}=\frac{-2 \pm \sqrt{4+16}}{2(4)}=\frac{-2 \pm \sqrt{20}}{8}=\frac{2- \pm 2 \sqrt{5}}{8}=\frac{-1 \pm \sqrt{5}}{4} \\
& \therefore \sin \mathrm{A}=\frac{-1+\sqrt{5}}{4} \text { or } \sin \mathrm{A}=\frac{-1+\sqrt{5}}{4}
\end{aligned}
$$
Since, $\sin \mathrm{A}>0, \sin \mathrm{A}=\frac{\sqrt{5}-1}{4}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of<math><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mfrac><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mfrac><mrow><mn>7</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Given expression can be written as

1+cos⁡π81+cos⁡7π81+cos⁡3π81+cos⁡5π8

=1+cosπ81-cosπ81+cos3π81-cos3π8

=1-cos2π81-cos23π8

=sin2π8sin23π8

= sin 2 π 8 · cos 2 π 8

= 1 4 2 sin π 8 cos π 8 2

= 1 4 sin 2 π 4

= 1 8
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\sin (y+z-x), \sin (z+x-y)$ and $\sin (x+y-z)$ are in A.P., then
questions.solution:
As $\sin (y+z-x), \sin (z+x-y)$ and $\sin (x+y-z)$ are in A.P.
$\therefore \sin (z+x-y)-\sin (y+z-x)=\sin (x+y-z)-\sin (z+x-y)$
$\Rightarrow 2 \cos z \sin (x-y)=2 \cos x \sin (y-z)$
$\Rightarrow \cos z \sin (x-y)=\cos x \sin (y-z)$
$\Rightarrow \frac{\cos z \sin (x-y)}{\cos x \cos y \cos z}=\frac{\cos x \sin (y-z)}{\cos x \cos y \cos z}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{\sin (x-y)}{\cos x \cos y}=\frac{\sin (y-z)}{\cos y \cos z}$
Using $\frac{\sin (A-B)}{\cos A \cos B}=\tan A-\tan B$
$\Rightarrow \tan x-\tan y=\tan y-\operatorname{taz}$
$\Rightarrow \tan y-\tan x=\tan z-\tan y$
$\therefore \tan x, \tan y \operatorname{and} \tan z \operatorname{are}$ in A.P.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If the value of <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>tan</mi><mi> </mi><msup><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>o</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>tan</mi><msup><mrow><mi> </mi><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>o</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>tan</mi><mi> </mi><msup><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>o</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></math><math><mo>…</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>tan</mi><msup><mrow><mi> </mi><mn>44</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>o</mn></mrow></msup><mo>)</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>tan</mi><mi> </mi><msup><mrow><mn>45</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>o</mn></mrow></msup><mo>)</mo></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>2</mn><mi>λ</mi></msup>  </math><math><mo>,</mo></math> then the sum of the digits of the number <math> <mi>λ</mi> </math> is
questions.solution:
If, A+B=45o

tan⁡A+B=1
∵tan(A+B)= tanA+tanB 1−tanAtanB

⇒tan⁡A+tan⁡B=1-tan⁡Atan⁡B

⇒ 1+tanA (1+tanB) = 2

LHS = 1+tan 1 o ) (1+tan 44 o )   (1+tan 2 o ) 1+tan 43 o ) …[ 1+tan 45 o ) for each 1+tanθ 1+tan π 4 −θ =2
= 2 22   1+1

=223

= 2 λ

then, λ=23. 

Hence the sum of digits of λ is 2+3

=5
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $$\frac{\cos 12^{\circ}-\sin 12^{\circ}}{\cos 12^{\circ}+\sin 12^{\circ}}+\frac{\sin 147^{\circ}}{\cos 147^{\circ}}=$$
questions.solution:
$\frac{\cos 12^{\circ}-\sin 12^{\circ}}{\cos 12^{\circ}+\sin 12^{\circ}}+\frac{\sin 147^{\circ}}{\cos 147^{\circ}}$
$=\frac{\cos 12^{\circ}-\cos 78^{\circ}}{\cos 12^{\circ}+\cos 78^{\circ}}+\tan 147^{\circ}$
$=\frac{-2 \sin 45^{\circ} \sin \left(-33^{\circ}\right)}{2 \cos 45^{\circ} \cos 33^{\circ}}+\tan \left(180^{\circ}-33^{\circ}\right)=\tan 45^{\circ} \tan 33^{\circ}-\tan 33^{\circ}$
$=\tan 33^{\circ}-\tan 33^{\circ}=0$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math><mi>a</mi><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">cos</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow><mo>=</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>b</mi><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow></mrow><mo>+</mo><mi>a</mi><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">cos</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow></mrow><mo>=</mo><mi>d</mi></math> and <math><mi>a</mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">tan</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow><mo>=</mo><mi>b</mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">tan</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow></mrow><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>b</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to (Here <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>c</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>d</mi><mo> </mo></math>are distinct)
questions.solution:
Dividing the given equations by cos2⁡x & cos2⁡y respectively. We get,
tan2x=c−ba−c and tan2y=d−ab−d

⇒a2b2=tan2ytan2x=(a−d) (c−a)(b−c) (d−b)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of the expression <math><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mn>1</mn><mo>°</mo><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>°</mo><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mn>3</mn><mo>°</mo><mo>+</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mn>89</mn><mo>°</mo><mo>)</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mn>1</mn><mo>°</mo><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>°</mo><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mn>3</mn><mo>°</mo><mo>+</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mn>44</mn><mo>°</mo><mo>)</mo><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
2sin1o+sin89o+sin2o+sin88o+.......+sin44o+sin46o+sin45o2cos1o+cos2o+cos3o+.......+cos44o+1

=22sin45ocos44o+cos43o+.......+cos1o+sin45o2cos1o+cos2o+cos3o+.......+cos44o+1

=2sin45o2cos1o+cos2o+.......+cos44o+12cos1o+cos2o+.......+cos44o+1

=2⋅12

=2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math> <math><mfenced close="}" open="{" separators="|"><mrow><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">tan</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo></mrow></mrow><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">cot</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow></mrow></mfenced><mo>.</mo></math> Then the number of roots of the equation <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></math> in <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0,2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is
questions.solution:
From the graph of tanx and cotx, we see that the range of fx is -1,0∪1,∞.
Hence, fx=max tan⁡x, cot⁡x and gx=12 do not cut each other
⇒ The number of solutions is zero.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>⁡</mo><msup><mn>71</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>⁡</mo><msup><mn>49</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mo>⁡</mo><msup><mn>71</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup><mi>cos</mi><mo>⁡</mo><msup><mn>49</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math><mn>10</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
E=1+cos⁡142o2+1+cos⁡98o2+122cos⁡71ocos⁡49o
=1+12cos⁡142o+cos⁡98o+12cos⁡120o+cos⁡22o
=1+122cos⁡120ocos⁡22o+12-12+cos⁡22o
=1+-12cos⁡22o-14+12cos⁡22o=34
E=34=0.75
⇒10E=7.5
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The solution set of $(5+4 \cos \theta)(2 \cos \theta+1)=0$ in the interval $[0,2 \pi]$, is :
questions.solution:
We have,
$(5+4 \cos \theta)(2 \cos \theta+1)=0$...(i)
$\cos \theta=\frac{1-\tan ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}}{1+\tan ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}}$
$\therefore \quad \cos \theta=\frac{1-t^2}{1+t^2} \quad\left[\right.$ put $\left.\tan \frac{\theta}{2}=t\right]$
Then, Eq. (i) becomes
$\left[5+4\left(\frac{1-t^2}{1+t^2}\right)\right]\left[2\left(\frac{1-t^2}{1+t^2}\right)+1\right]=0$
$\Rightarrow\left[5+5 t^2+4-4 t^2\right]\left[2-2 t^2+1+t^2\right]=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad\left(t^2+9\right)\left(3-t^2\right)=0$
$\therefore \quad t= \pm \sqrt{3}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \tan \frac{\theta}{2}=\sqrt{3}$ or $\tan \frac{\theta}{2}=-\sqrt{3}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{\pi}{3}$ or $\frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{2 \pi}{3}$
$\therefore \quad \theta=\frac{2 \pi}{3}$ or $\frac{4 \pi}{3}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: <math><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><msup><mrow><mn>60</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>o</mi></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><msup><mrow><mn>80</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>o</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><msup><mrow><mn>10</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>o</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo></math> ….
questions.solution:
We have, 1-2cos60o-cos80o2sin10o
=1-212-cos80o2sin10o
=2cos80o2sin10o
=cos⁡90o-10osin10o
=sin10osin10o=1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: <math><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mn>3</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0.4</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mrow><mo>=</mo></math> ….
questions.solution:
Let E=sin⁡3sin-10.4
put sin-10.4=θ⇒sinθ=0.4
∴E=sin⁡3θ=3sinθ-4sin3θ
=30.4-40.43
=12-40.064
=12-0.256
=0.944

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Consider the function defined by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow><msqrt><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>⁡</mo><mi>x</mi></msqrt></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow><msqrt><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>cot</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>⁡</mo><mi>x</mi></msqrt></mfrac></math> for permissible values. Then which of the following is true?
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>sin</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mi>x</mi><mfenced open="|" close="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mi>cos</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mi>x</mi><mfenced open="|" close="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> for all <math><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math>
questions.solution:
(i), (ii) and (iv) are clearly false, since fx is not defined for kπ2 , on the contrary the two RHS included in choices (i), (ii) and (iv) are defined for all x.

(iii) is true since fx=sin⁡xcos⁡x|+cos⁡xsin⁡x|

fx=sin⁡xcos⁡x-cos⁡xsin⁡x=0 for π2<x<π
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\tan A+2 \tan 2 A+4 \tan 4 A+8 \operatorname{Cot} 8 A=$
questions.solution:
(C)
$=\tan \mathrm{A}+2 \tan 2 \mathrm{~A}+4 \tan 4 \mathrm{~A}+8 \times \frac{1-\tan ^{2} 4 \mathrm{~A}}{2 \tan 4 \mathrm{~A}}$
$=\tan \mathrm{A}+2 \tan 2 \mathrm{~A}+4 \tan 4 \mathrm{~A}+\frac{4\left(1-\tan ^{2} 4 \mathrm{~A}\right)}{\tan 4 \mathrm{~A}}$
$=\tan \mathrm{A}+2 \tan 2 \mathrm{~A}+\frac{4 \tan ^{2} 4 \mathrm{~A}+4-4 \tan ^{2} 4 \mathrm{~A}}{\tan 4 \mathrm{~A}}$
$=\tan \mathrm{A}+2 \tan 2 \mathrm{~A}+\frac{4}{\tan 4 \mathrm{~A}}=\tan \mathrm{A}+2 \tan 2 \mathrm{~A}+4 \cot 4 \mathrm{~A}$
$=\tan \mathrm{A}+2 \tan 2 \mathrm{~A}+4 \times \frac{1-\tan ^{2} 2 \mathrm{~A}}{2 \tan 2 \mathrm{~A}}=\tan \mathrm{A}+2 \tan 2 \mathrm{~A}+\frac{2\left(1-\tan ^{2} 2 \mathrm{~A}\right)}{\tan 2 \mathrm{~A}}$
$=\tan \mathrm{A}+\frac{2 \tan ^{2} 2 \mathrm{~A}+2-2 \tan ^{2} 2 \mathrm{~A}}{\tan 2 \mathrm{~A}}=\tan \mathrm{A}+\frac{2}{\tan 2 \mathrm{~A}}$
$=\tan \mathrm{A}+2 \cot 2 \mathrm{~A}=\frac{2\left(1-\tan ^{2} \mathrm{~A}\right)}{2 \tan \mathrm{A}}=\tan \mathrm{A}+\frac{1-\tan ^{2} \mathrm{~A}}{\tan \mathrm{A}}$
$=\frac{\tan ^{2} \mathrm{~A}+1-\tan ^{2} \mathrm{~A}}{\tan \mathrm{A}}=\frac{1}{\tan \mathrm{A}}=\cot \mathrm{A}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $x=\tan 15^{\circ}, y=\operatorname{cosec} 75^{\circ}$ and $z=4 \sin 18^{\circ}$, then :
questions.solution:
$\because \quad x=\tan 15^{\circ}=\tan\left(45^{\circ}-30^{\circ}\right)$
$=\frac{\tan 45^{\circ}-\tan 30^{\circ}}{1+\tan 45^{\circ} \tan 30^{\circ}}=\frac{1-\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}{1+\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}$
$=\frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{\sqrt{3}+1}=\frac{(\sqrt{3}-1)^2}{3-1}$
$=\frac{3+1-2 \sqrt{3}}{2}=2-\sqrt{3}$
and $y=\operatorname{cosec} 75^{\circ}$
$=\frac{1}{\sin \left(45^{\circ}+30^{\circ}\right)}$
$=\frac{1}{\sin 45^{\circ} \cos 30^{\circ}+\cos 45^{\circ} \sin 30^{\circ}}$
$=\frac{1}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2 \sqrt{2}}+\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}}}=\frac{2 \sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}+1} \times \frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{\sqrt{3}-1}$
$=\frac{2 \sqrt{2}(\sqrt{3}-1)}{3-1}=\sqrt{6}-\sqrt{2}$
and $\quad z=4 \sin 18^{\circ}=4\left(\frac{\sqrt{5}-1}{4}\right)=\sqrt{5}-1$
It is clear from above that
$(2-\sqrt{3}) < (\sqrt{6}-\sqrt{2}) < (\sqrt{5}-1)$
$\Rightarrow \quad x < y < z$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $A=35^{\circ}, B=15^{\circ}$ and $C=40^{\circ}$, then $\tan A \cdot \tan B+\tan B \cdot \tan C+\tan C \cdot \tan A$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given that, $A=35^{\circ}, B=15^{\circ}$ and $C=40^{\circ}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \tan (A+B+C) \\
& =\frac{\left[\begin{array}{c}
\tan A+\tan B+\tan C \\
-\tan A \tan B \tan C
\end{array}\right]}{\left[\begin{array}{c}
1-\tan A \tan B-\tan B \tan C \\
-\tan C \tan A
\end{array}\right]} \\
& \Rightarrow \tan \left(90^{\circ}\right) \\
& =\frac{\tan A+\tan B+\tan C-\tan A \tan B \tan C}{1-\tan A \tan B-\tan B \tan C-\tan C \tan A} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \tan A \tan B+\tan B \tan C \\
& +\tan C \tan A=1 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In a triangle $\mathrm{ABC}$, with usual notations, if $\mathrm{c}=4$, then value of $(a-b)^2 \cos ^2 \frac{C}{2}+(a+b)^2 \sin ^2 \frac{C}{2}$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & (a-b)^2 \cos ^2 \frac{C}{2}+(a+b)^2 \sin ^2 \frac{C}{2} \\ & =\left(a^2-2 a b+b^2\right) \cos ^2 \frac{C}{2}+\left(a^2+2 a b+b^2\right) \sin ^2 \frac{C}{2} \\ & =\left(a^2+b^2\right)\left(\cos ^2 \frac{C}{2}+\sin ^2 \frac{C}{2}\right) \\ & \quad-2 a b \cos ^2 \frac{C}{2}+2 a b \sin ^2 \frac{C}{2} \\ & =a^2+b^2-2 a b\left(\cos ^2 \frac{C}{2}-\sin ^2 \frac{C}{2}\right) \\ & =a^2+b^2-2 a b \cdot \cos C \\ & =a^2+b^2-\left(a^2+b^2-c^2\right) \quad \ldots[\text { By Cosine rule }] \\ & =c^2=4^2=16\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\tan \theta+\tan \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)+\tan \left(\theta+\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right)=3$, then which of the following is equal to 1 ?
questions.solution:
Given,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \tan \theta+\tan \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)+\tan \left(\theta+\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right)=3 \\
& \Rightarrow \tan \theta+\frac{\tan \theta+\sqrt{3}}{1-\sqrt{3} \tan \theta}+\frac{\tan \theta-\sqrt{3}}{1+\sqrt{3} \tan \theta}=3 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \tan \theta+\frac{8 \tan \theta}{1-3 \tan ^2 \theta}=3 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \frac{9 \tan \theta-3 \tan ^3 \theta}{1-3 \tan ^2 \theta}=3 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 3 \tan 3 \theta=3 \Rightarrow \tan 3 \theta=1 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, option (2) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\quad$ The maximum value of the function $y=e^{5+\sqrt{3} \sin x+\cos x}$ is
questions.solution:
Maximum value of $\sqrt{3} \sin x+\cos x$ is $\sqrt{(\sqrt{3})^{2}+(1)^{2}}=2$ 
Hence maximum value of given function is $\mathrm{e}^{5+2}=\mathrm{e}^{7}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\frac{\cos x}{\cos (x-2 y)}=\lambda \Rightarrow \tan (x-y) \tan y$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
tan(x-y) \tan y=\frac{\sin (x-y) \sin y}{\cos (x-y) \cos y} \times \frac{2}{2} \\
=\frac{\cos (x-2 y)-\cos (x)}{\cos (x-2 y)+\cos (x)} \\
=\frac{1-\frac{\cos x}{\cos (x-2 y)}}{1+\frac{\cos (x)}{\cos (x-2 y)}} \\
=\frac{1-\lambda}{1+\lambda} \\
&\left(\text { Given, } \lambda=\frac{\cos x}{\cos (x-2 y)}\right)
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The number of solutions in $[0,2 \pi]$ of the equation $16^{\sin ^2 x}+16^{\cos ^2 x}=10$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & 16^{\sin ^2 x}+16^{\cos ^2 x}=10 \\ & 16^{\sin ^2 x}+16^{1-\sin ^2 x}=10 \\ & 16^{\sin ^2 x}+\frac{16}{16^{\sin ^2 x}}=10\end{aligned}$
Let $16^{\sin ^2 x}=\mathrm{t}$
$\begin{array}{ll}
\therefore & \mathrm{t}+\frac{16}{\mathrm{t}}=10 \\
\therefore & \mathrm{t}^2-10 \mathrm{t}+16=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}=2 \text { and } \mathrm{t}=8
\end{array}$
Now, $16^{\sin ^2 x}=2$ and $16^{\sin ^2 x}=8$
$\begin{aligned}
& 2^{4 \sin ^2 x}=2^1 \text { and } 2^{4 \sin ^2 x}=2^3 \\
\therefore \quad & 4 \sin ^2 x=1 \text { and } 4 \sin ^2 x=3 \\
\therefore \quad & \sin ^2 x=\frac{1}{4} \text { and } \sin ^2 x=\frac{3}{4} \\
& \sin x= \pm \frac{1}{2} \text { and } \sin x= \pm \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \\
\therefore \quad & x=\frac{\pi}{6}, \frac{5 \pi}{6}, \frac{7 \pi}{6}, \frac{11 \pi}{6} \text { and } x=\frac{\pi}{3}, \frac{2 \pi}{3}, \frac{4 \pi}{3}, \frac{5 \pi}{3}
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad$ number of solutions $=8$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\tan 81^{\circ}-\tan 63^{\circ}-\tan 27^{\circ}+\tan 9^{\circ}$ equals to
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \tan 81^{\circ}-\tan 63^{\circ}-\tan 27^{\circ}+\tan 9^{\circ} \\ & =\left(\tan 81^{\circ}+\tan 9^{\circ}\right)-\left[\tan \left(63^{\circ}\right)+\tan \left(27^{\circ}\right)\right] \\ & =\left[\frac{\sin 81^{\circ}}{\cos 81^{\circ}}+\frac{\sin 9^{\circ}}{\cos 9^{\circ}}\right]-\left[\frac{\sin 63^{\circ}}{\cos 63^{\circ}}+\frac{\sin 27^{\circ}}{\cos 27^{\circ}}\right] \\ & =\left[\frac{\sin 81^{\circ} \cos 9^{\circ}+\sin 9^{\circ} \cos 81^{\circ}}{\cos 9^{\circ} \cos 81^{\circ}}\right] \\ & -\left[\frac{\sin 63^{\circ} \cos 27^{\circ}+\sin 27^{\circ} \cos 63^{\circ}}{\cos 27^{\circ} \cos 63^{\circ}}\right] \\ & =\frac{\left[\sin \left(81^{\circ}+9^{\circ}\right)\right]}{\cos 9^{\circ} \cos \left(90^{\circ}-90^{\circ}\right)} \\ & =\frac{\left[\sin \left(63^{\circ}+27^{\circ}\right)\right]}{\cos 27^{\circ} \cos \left(90^{\circ}-27^{\circ}\right)} \\ & =\frac{1}{\sin 9^{\circ} \cos 9^{\circ}} \times \frac{2}{2}-\frac{1}{\sin 27^{\circ} \cos 27^{\circ}} \times \frac{2}{2} \\ & =\frac{2}{\cos 27^{\circ} \sin 27^{\circ}}-\frac{2}{\sin 54^{\circ}}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =2\left[\frac{\sin 54^{\circ}-\sin 18^{\circ}}{\sin 18^{\circ} \sin 54^{\circ}}\right] \\ & =2\left[\frac{2 \cos 36^{\circ} \sin 18^{\circ}}{\sin 18^{\circ} \cos 36^{\circ}}\right] \\ & =4\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><msub><mi>θ</mi><mi>n</mi></msub></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>n</mi><mo>.</mo></math> Then value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><msub><mi>θ</mi><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><msub><mi>θ</mi><mi>n</mi></msub></mfenced></math> is equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><msub><mi>θ</mi><mi>n</mi></msub></math> lies in <math><mo>[</mo><mn>0,90</mn><mo>°</mo><mo>]</mo></math>)
questions.solution:
Given, sinθ1+sinθ2+………..+sinθn=n

⇒sinθ1=sinθ2=sinθ3=………..=sinθn=1

⇒θ1=θ2………..=θn=90o

∴sinθ1+cosθ2+sinθ3+………..+sinθn-1+cos(θn)

=sinθ1+sinθ3+………..+sinθn-1+cosθ2+cosθ4+…+cosθn

=n2+0

=n2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\theta+\phi=\alpha$ and $\tan \theta=\mathrm{k} \tan \phi$ (where $\mathrm{K}>1)$, then the value of $\sin (\theta-\phi)$ is
questions.solution:
We have $\tan \theta=\mathrm{k} \tan \phi$ and $\theta+\phi=\alpha$
$$
\therefore \frac{\tan \theta}{\tan \phi}=\frac{k}{1}
$$
By Componendo Dividendo, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \frac{\tan \theta+\tan \phi}{\tan \theta-\tan \phi}=\frac{\mathrm{k}+1}{\mathrm{k}-1} \\
& \therefore \frac{\frac{\sin \theta}{\cos \theta}+\frac{\sin \phi}{\cos \phi}}{\frac{\sin \theta}{\cos \theta}-\frac{\sin \phi}{\cos \phi}}=\frac{k+1}{k-1} \\
& \therefore \frac{\sin \cos \phi+\cos \theta \sin \phi}{\sin \theta \cos \phi-\cos \theta \sin \phi}=\frac{k+1}{k-1} \\
& \therefore \frac{\sin (\theta+\phi)}{\sin (\theta-\phi)}=\frac{\mathrm{k}+1}{\mathrm{k}-1} \Rightarrow \frac{\sin \alpha}{\sin (\theta-\phi)}=\frac{\mathrm{k}+1}{\mathrm{k}-1} \\
& \therefore \sin (\theta-\phi)=\frac{\mathrm{k}-1}{\mathrm{k}+1}(\sin \alpha) \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of $\tan \frac{\pi}{8}$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { Since } \tan 2 \theta=\frac{2 \tan \theta}{1-\tan ^2 \theta} \\
& \therefore \quad \tan \frac{\pi}{4}=\frac{2 \tan \frac{\pi}{8}}{1-\tan ^2 \frac{\pi}{8}} \\
& \Rightarrow 1=\frac{2 \tan \frac{\pi}{8}}{1-\tan ^2 \frac{\pi}{8}} \\
& \text { Let } y=\tan \frac{\pi}{8} \\
& \Rightarrow 1=\frac{2 y}{1-y^2} \\
& \Rightarrow y^2+2 y-1=0 \\
& \Rightarrow y=\frac{-2 \pm \sqrt{4+4}}{2} \\
& \Rightarrow y=\frac{-2 \pm 2 \sqrt{2}}{2} \\
& \Rightarrow y=-1 \pm \sqrt{2} \\
& \tan \frac{\pi}{8}=-1 \pm \sqrt{2}
\end{aligned}$
Since $\frac{\pi}{8}$ lies in $1^{\text {st }}$ quadrant.
$\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad \tan \frac{\pi}{8} & \neq-1-\sqrt{2} \\
\therefore \quad \tan \frac{\pi}{8} & =-1+\sqrt{2} \\
& =\sqrt{2}-1
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The sum of the solutions in $(0,2 \pi)$ the equation $\cos x \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{3}-x\right) \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{3}+x\right)=\frac{1}{4}$ is
questions.solution:
We know,
$$
\cos x \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{3}-x\right) \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{3}+x\right)=\frac{1}{4} \cos 3 \theta
$$
But it is given
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \cos x \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{3}-x\right) \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{3}+x\right)=\frac{1}{4} \\
\therefore \quad \cos 3 \theta & =1 \\
\Rightarrow \quad 3 \theta & =2 \pi, 4 \pi \\
\Rightarrow \quad \theta & =\frac{2 \pi}{3}, \frac{4 \pi}{3}
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore$ Sum of solutions
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{2 \pi}{3}+\frac{4 \pi}{3}=\frac{6 \pi}{3} \\
& =2 \pi
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\cos ^2 76^{\circ}+\cos ^2 16^{\circ}-\cos 76^{\circ} \cos 16^{\circ}$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \cos ^2 76^{\circ}+\cos ^2 16^{\circ}-\cos 76^{\circ} \cos 16^{\circ} \\ & =\cos ^2 76^{\circ}+1-\sin ^2 16^{\circ}-\frac{1}{2} \cdot 2 \cos 76^{\circ} \cos 16^{\circ} \\ & =1+\cos \left(76^{\circ}+16^{\circ}\right) \cos \left(76^{\circ}-16^{\circ}\right) \\ & \quad-\frac{1}{2}\left(\cos 92^{\circ}+\cos 60^{\circ}\right) \\ & =1+\frac{1}{2} \cos 92^{\circ}-\frac{1}{2} \cos 92^{\circ}-\frac{1}{4} \\ & =1-\frac{1}{4}=\frac{3}{4}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\cos A=\frac{7}{25}$ and $\frac{3 \pi}{2} < A < 2 \pi$, then $\cos \frac{A}{4}+\cos \frac{A}{2}-\cos 2 A=$
questions.solution:
Given, $\quad \cos A=\frac{7}{25}$ and $\frac{3 \pi}{2} < A < 2 \pi$
Then, $\quad \cos 2 A=2\left(\frac{49}{625}\right)-1=\frac{98-625}{625}=-\frac{527}{625}$ $\cos \frac{A}{2}=-\frac{4}{5}$ and $\cos \frac{A}{4}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{10}}$
So, $\quad \cos \frac{A}{4}+\cos \frac{A}{2}-\cos 2 A=\frac{1}{\sqrt{10}}+\frac{27}{625}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
